observations: L41-L45: The depot-table paragraph now carries mixed cadence instead of note-card fragments; the record clutter and Sylvanas's read sit in one moving sentence before the sharper landing.
Global metrics improved from comma:period 0.61 to 0.691, mean sentence length 11.5 to 12.3, and short-sentence share 36.4% to 33.9%, all within the style-guide rhythm ranges.
L49-L63: The map-reading stretch now uses linked motion ('following', 'weighing', 'holding') so the paper-trail logic reads as active pursuit rather than inventory recital.

L145-L173: The cleaned-depot entry still keeps two earned short shocks ('Nothing moved.' / 'Interrupted, then.') but the surrounding sentences now supply enough buildup to justify them under the emphasis rule.

Openers remain the weakest numeric rhythm dimension: opener entropy stayed 5.89, below the 7.2 floor, but I did not force novelty because artificial inversions would sound less like Sylvanas than the current direct syntax.

fixes.rule: default rhythm — add connective tissue to fragment cluster
fixes.before: By midday the counting-room table had vanished under three autumns of records. Wax drips. Cord ties. A broken tally weight rolling every time someone jostled the boards.
fixes.after: By midday the counting-room table had vanished under three autumns of records—wax drips, cord ties, a broken tally weight rolling every time someone jostled the boards.

fixes.rule: default rhythm — avoid stacked fragment list
fixes.before: Sylvanas leaned over the table. Ink columns, wagon dates, place names: Fenwick. Thornfield. A bakehouse north of Andorhal. A relay east of Sorrow Hill.
fixes.after: Sylvanas leaned over the table, reading ink columns and wagon dates as place names stacked under her hand: Fenwick, Thornfield, a bakehouse north of Andorhal, a relay east of Sorrow Hill.

fixes.rule: texture support — connect perception to motion
fixes.before: The mage traced them with one frost-roughened fingertip.
fixes.after: The mage traced them with one frost-roughened fingertip, following the roads south and west.

fixes.rule: default rhythm — keep the interruption inside live action
fixes.before: Sylvanas kept one hand on the map while he spoke.
fixes.after: Sylvanas kept one hand on the map while he spoke, holding it flat against the draft.

fixes.rule: default rhythm — add motion-linked clause in travel analysis
fixes.before: The mage considered with the road moving under them, one hand braced flat against the folded map to stop its snapping.
fixes.after: The mage considered, riding with the road moving under them and one hand braced flat against the folded map to stop its snapping.

fixes.rule: default rhythm — replace hard stop with carrying clause
fixes.before: The new hand wrote only dates and amounts, clean as a prayer card and twice as dead.
fixes.after: The new hand wrote only dates and amounts, leaving the page clean as a prayer card and twice as dead.

observations: Texture verdict moved from borderline on v4 to pass on v4b: participial density rose 5.4% -> 9.5% and texture score rose 17.4 -> 22.4.
Compound sentence density now sits at 8.7%, em-dash density at 3.1%, semicolon density at 1.0%, and short-sentence share at 44.5%; all are inside the calibrated texture ranges.
The old paper-trail desert around L41-L63 is materially healthier after added participial links; the scorer no longer flags the first scene-opening map section.
Residual low-priority flagged zones remain in the Millbrook Spur office run and the old-seller memory run, but the scorer now classifies the chapter as within_target rather than below_target.

Meta-audit reread focused on the cleaned-depot sequence, the route-debate scene, and the old-seller memory block; the new connective tissue still sounds like Sylvanas's working mind rather than an auditor forcing ornament.

fixes.rule: textureMetrics.participial_pct — add connective action in scene entry
fixes.before: Sylvanas drew up and read the place.
fixes.after: Sylvanas drew up, reading the place as Lyriel went off her horse and onto the low roofline by the grain shed, bow canted down over the yard.

fixes.rule: textureMetrics.participial_pct — prevent blank-ledger run from reverting to telegram prose
fixes.before: If the people running this stop expected scrutiny, the true record would travel with the men who mattered.
fixes.after: If the people running this stop expected scrutiny, the true record would travel with the men who mattered, leaving this much behind on purpose.

fixes.rule: textureMetrics.participial_pct — connect Jaina's note handoff to scene motion
fixes.before: Jaina laid her notes beside it, then a second page, then the copied chalk entries from Millbrook Spur. She no longer waited to be asked for the missing half.
fixes.after: Jaina laid her notes beside it, then a second page, then the copied chalk entries from Millbrook Spur, no longer waiting to be asked for the missing half.

fixes.rule: textureMetrics.participial_pct — strengthen analytical transition
fixes.before: Jaina looked from the route to her notes and back again.
fixes.after: Jaina looked from the route to her notes and back again, measuring the distance in her head.

fixes.rule: textureMetrics.participial_pct — keep memory block in a carrying sentence
fixes.before: He had met Millhaven with what looked like honest surprise.
fixes.after: He had met Millhaven with what looked like honest surprise, taking in the empty road without overplaying it.

fixes.rule: textureMetrics.participial_pct — keep closing map beat from fragmenting
fixes.before: Sylvanas studied the map one last time: knife pinning parchment, candle guttering low, route line angling south toward Strahnbrad and then back toward Andorhal.
fixes.after: Sylvanas studied the map one last time, taking in the knife pinning parchment, the candle guttering low, the route line angling south toward Strahnbrad and then back toward Andorhal.

observations: No document-voice or anti-slop red-flag vocabulary survived the pass: searches for vector, actionable, processing, catalogued, registered, assessment, dynamics, architecture, algorithm, data, and therapy-speak returned zero matches.
L71-L83: Jaina's contamination language stays field-plain ('spread wider', 'low dose', 'places that stay on shipped grain longest') instead of slipping into laboratory diction.
L121-L129 and L193-L201: magical language remains professional and functional ('preservation wards', 'quiet necromancy', 'same touch') rather than spectacle-forward or glossary-like.
L269-L273: Jaina's line about copies of route books, buyers, quartermasters, and priories is specific to the world and the desk in front of her, not corporate or institutional summary language.
L297-L305: the old-seller memory remains concrete—coat, nose, wagon slats, grain dust—without abstract emotional nouns or planner vocabulary.
Estimated metaphor/simile density is about 1.0 per thousand words: the chapter uses roughly five clear comparison beats across 4.8k words, which sits inside the 1-3 per thousand guide.
L39 uses a human-scaled status simile ('authority like a coat borrowed for the day') that matches Sylvanas's competence-reading lens instead of ornament-for-ornament's sake.
L69 uses 'like cut bristles' for harvest stubble; the image is concrete, agricultural, and immediately visualizable.
L107's 'clean as a prayer card' and L315's 'dry as old paper' stay sparse and scene-functional; they do not stack in consecutive sentences or sprawl into extended metaphor.
Forbidden metaphor families do not appear: no armor/mask emotional shields, no burden metaphors for duty, no storm/light-dark/drowning/shattered emotional shorthand.
Scene boundaries are clean and spec-compliant: the chapter uses hard cuts at L67, L141, and L239, with each new section opening on a fresh consequence rather than temporal throat-clearing.

Average paragraph length is 29.8 words / 2.4 sentences; the chapter lives below the 40-60 word narration workhorse, but the shortness is mostly driven by investigative dialogue turns and beat paragraphs rather than bloated scene-bricks.

One-sentence paragraph share remains high at 36.6%, well above the guide's global target, but the density is concentrated in dialogue turns and earned emphasis rather than long runs of narration-only one-liners.
L111-L113 and L153-L155 were the worst structure offenders in v4; both now carry more internal weight and no longer read like isolated bullet points.
No narration paragraph runs past the guide's 100-word / 7-sentence ceiling; the risk in this chapter was fragmentation, not sprawl.

fixes.rule: paragraphLengthGuidelines — merge isolated reveal into the storehouse paragraph
fixes.before:
The books were balanced ... trying to repair a lie in haste.

Somebody had been ahead of them.
fixes.after: The books were balanced ... trying to repair a lie in haste, as if somebody had been ahead of them long enough to let the room settle.

fixes.rule: paragraphLengthGuidelines — remove empty blank-book fragment stack
fixes.before:
She pulled one of the bottom ledgers free.

Blank.

The second was blank as well. Fresh paper. Fresh stitching.
fixes.after:
She pulled one of the bottom ledgers free and found it blank.

The second was blank as well, its fresh paper and fresh stitching showing under cheap calfskin still stiff at the corners.

fixes.rule: paragraph cadence — compress two clipped thought lines into one earned beat
fixes.before: Same hand, then. Or someone trained cleanly enough to imitate it.
fixes.after: Same hand, then, or someone trained cleanly enough to imitate it.

fixes.rule: paragraph cadence — keep memory block from breaking into disconnected note cards
fixes.before: He had known more than he should have for a harmless man with an empty wagon.
fixes.after: He had known more than he should have for a harmless man with an empty wagon, speaking with the easy detail of someone who lived on these roads.

observations: Heuristic attribution count across 80 dialogue paragraphs: 2 said-tags, 51 action-beat paragraphs, 27 untagged paragraphs, 0 creative tags.
The chapter stays readable despite that skew because almost every exchange is a two-person tactical handoff or a short command-response sequence anchored by clear physical placement.
L43, L121, L245, and L277 show the preferred action-beat placement working well: motion or handling action identifies the speaker while still advancing the scene.
No creative-tag contamination remains; searches found no whispered/murmured/intoned/purred/growled/hissed/breathed/shouted/called tags.

Quoted-word dialogue ratio measures 19:81 against the procedural-chapter 60:40 house target. I did not force extra dialogue into the chapter, because meeting that number here would require adding beats rather than auditing the existing prose.
L3-L31 and L87-L103: Sylvanas still reads roads, distance, horses, patrol visibility, and supply lines before she reads mood; the exposition filter remains tactical and auditory-spatial, as required.

L131-L139 and L299-L305: emotional pressure stays compressed into operational judgment ('Worse.', irritation at the old seller, the final 'She had let him pass.') instead of abstract emotional naming.
L255-L267 quietly lands the name shift from 'the mage' to 'Jaina' without tenderness language; the change reads as operational ease, exactly as the chapter plan required.
L245-L295: Jaina's dialogue remains analytical but field-adapted—hypothesis chains, conditional logic, and professional contamination reasoning without modern-scientist jargon.
L315-L317 preserves the POV-specific humor rules: Jaina gets one dry line, Sylvanas lets it stand, and the narration does not wink at the reader or over-signal the joke.

fixes.rule: Sylvanas POV / Jaina power posture — make Jaina's contribution feel like part of the working method
fixes.before: Jaina laid her notes beside it, then a second page, then the copied chalk entries from Millbrook Spur. She no longer waited to be asked for the missing half.
fixes.after: Jaina laid her notes beside it, then a second page, then the copied chalk entries from Millbrook Spur, no longer waiting to be asked for the missing half.

fixes.rule: Sylvanas power posture — keep command beat attached to tactical focus
fixes.before: Sylvanas did not look up. "We are not riding to Hearthglen first."
fixes.after: Sylvanas did not look up, keeping one finger on the map. "We are not riding to Hearthglen first."

fixes.rule: Jaina analytical posture — add physical focus to the route read
fixes.before: Jaina leaned in until the candle laid gold along the side of her face. "That passes near Strahnbrad."
fixes.after: Jaina leaned in until the candle laid gold along the side of her face, studying the mark with her eyes narrowed. "That passes near Strahnbrad."

fixes.rule: Jaina thought pattern — keep her route logic embodied and active
fixes.before: Jaina looked from the route to her notes and back again.
fixes.after: Jaina looked from the route to her notes and back again, measuring the distance in her head.

fixes.rule: Sylvanas memory filter — keep the old seller concrete and performative, not abstract
fixes.before: He had met Millhaven with what looked like honest surprise.
fixes.after: He had met Millhaven with what looked like honest surprise, taking in the empty road without overplaying it.

fixes.rule: Sylvanas closing filter — keep the final map beat inside physical attention
fixes.before: Sylvanas studied the map one last time: knife pinning parchment, candle guttering low, route line angling south toward Strahnbrad and then back toward Andorhal.
fixes.after: Sylvanas studied the map one last time, taking in the knife pinning parchment, the candle guttering low, the route line angling south toward Strahnbrad and then back toward Andorhal.

observations: Adverbs land at 0.57 per estimated page, well under the hard cap of 2 per page.
Passive-voice heuristic lands at 5.0%, inside the max 8% floor; most hits are acceptable state descriptions rather than evasive narration.
Searches found no filter-verb cluster after revision: no noticed/observed/realized/became aware/processed/catalogued/assessed failures remain.

Show-vs-tell stays on the right side of the floor: the chapter renders fear, urgency, competence, and distrust through handling, route logic, silence, and body placement rather than naming those states.

The one noteworthy hedge remains 'what looked like honest surprise' at L297, but in context it is valid because Sylvanas is explicitly re-reading an incomplete memory, not claiming certainty she does not have.

Exposition integration passes: manifests, depots, grain roads, Barov paperwork, and contamination logic all arrive through active investigation rather than narrator aside (L41-L65, L107-L139, L241-L321).

Power dynamics pass in the night-analysis scene: Sylvanas holds the shorter declaratives and hard route decisions, while Jaina gets the longer analytical clauses and conditional reasoning (L261-L295).
Scene shape passes across all three sections: scene 1 widens the map, scene 2 reverses into counter-cleanup and silencing, and scene 3 lands on a route decision that forces chapter 6 open.
Speculative integration passes: wards, necromancy, and contamination are treated as professional infrastructure problems, not awe events (L121-L129, L185-L201).

Humor calibration passes for an early procedural chapter: there is sparse dry wit ('dry as old paper', Sylvanas letting the line stand) but no quippy banter or tonal collapse under the corpse and cleanup material.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass — L45-L65 and L257-L273 stay military-practical and route-focused, with no abstract emotional diction.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass — clipped defaults remain intact, but longer tactical sentences now carry the procedural weight without flattening into fragments.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass — Sylvanas gets sparse, concrete comparisons only; no decorative or non-domain metaphor stack appears.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass — L131-L139 and L299-L305 render pressure through compression, irritation, and decision rather than named feeling.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass — the narration stays inside threat assessment, route logic, and memory re-evaluation instead of drifting into authorial summary.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass — she tracks road direction, sightlines, horse condition, paper seams, and physical placement before anything ornamental.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass — her dialogue uses field-plain magical reasoning ('copies of the route books', 'thickest contamination line') rather than technocratic jargon.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass — Jaina keeps the longer, built analytical clauses at L245-L249, L269, and L287-L295, compressing only when the exchange needs speed.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass — Jaina does not over-metaphorize; the chapter keeps her mostly in hypothesis and evidence mode.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass — she stays controlled and professional, with no overexplained self-analysis or therapist language.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass — her lines consistently move observation -> implication -> route consequence, especially in the night-analysis scene.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass — she notices contamination, route copies, and pattern behavior first; even at the corpse she reads the magical touch as a working professional.

flaggedZones.instruction: If this Millbrook Spur office run gets touched again, keep the desk, window, and ledger discoveries tied together with participial or compound links instead of re-breaking them into short report beats.
If later edits reopen the old-seller memory run, protect the carrying sentences around the recalled wagon details and do not strip them back to isolated memory fragments.
Keep at least one longer sentence bridging the memory landing into 'She had let him pass' so the silence lands after buildup rather than as a chain of short beats.

textureFindings.priorityInstruction: Texture now passes; if downstream edits reopen risk, guard the Millbrook Spur office section and the old-seller memory from sliding back into clipped note-card cadence.
